title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays — O(n+m)
description:
Given two sorted arrays `a` and `b`, return a single sorted array containing all elements of both.

**The point**: don't concat-and-sort (that's O((n+m) log(n+m))). A two-pointer merge runs in **O(n+m)** — linear in the total size.
hints: Sorting via concat: [...a, ...b].sort() works but is O((n+m) log(n+m)). We can do better.
Since both inputs are already sorted, we just need to interleave them.
Two pointers, one into each array, advancing whichever points at the smaller element.

testCases.description: all of b before all of a
